title: Middleware authorization bypass via App Router segment-prefetch/.rsc transport routes in Next.js
description: Next.js builds the regex matchers that determine which requests run user middleware in getMiddlewareMatchers() (get-page-static-info.ts). For each configured matcher source the function appends an optional suffix group that only accounts for the Pages Router data-route transport form `.json` (and, for the root, `/index`/`/index.json`); it does not include the App Router transport variants `.rsc` and the segment-prefetch form `...segments/<seg>.segment.rsc`. As a result, a `.rsc` or segment-prefetch URL resolves to the same protected page but is not matched by the generated middleware matcher, so middleware-based authorization checks are skipped. App Router applications that rely on middleware (or proxy-based checks) for authorization can have protected content reached via these crafted transport URLs without the expected authorization check.
